programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Kate Winslett
programme.desc: Acclaimed movie star Kate Winslet takes a role like never before, as Bear paramotors them both to the top of the Snowdonia Mountains in Wales.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Deon Sanders
programme.desc: Bear Grylls takes NFL hall of famer Deion Sanders to the mesas and canyons of Utah for his first ever camping trip.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, President Barack Obama
programme.desc: President Barack Obama and Bear Grylls explore the Alaskan wilderness, where they observe evidence of climate change, sample wild salmon and catkins tea made with water from melting glaciers; Obama discusses his official duties, parenthood and faith.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Uzo Aduba
programme.desc: Uzo Aduba joins Bear on a harrowing journey to Spain's Pyrenees Mountains, where they face a poisonous viper and secure a dinner of field mice; Uzo shares the story of her path to fame and her family's emigration from Nigeria.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: 7 Celebrities,7 Days, 7 Craziest Challenges with Bear Grylls starting: Michelle Rodriguez
programme.desc: Actress Michelle Rodriguez and Bear go to a desert Nevada; unexpected high winds during a sky-diving trip send Michelle off course; Michelle and Bear must come up with clever ways to stay hydrated.
programme.sub-title: 7 Celebrities,7 Days, 7 Craziest Challenges with Bear Grylls starting: Michelle Rodriguez

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Joseph Gordon-Levitt
programme.desc: Bear and Joseph Gordon-Levitt navigate Kenya's unyielding terrain; Joseph and Bear navigate a perilous rappel, milk a camel for hydration, ascend a massive rock dome and battle a crocodile for dinner; Joseph talks about his family and rise to fame.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Scott Eastwood
programme.desc: Actor Scott Eastwood joins Bear on a journey through the harsh Bulgarian outback; the duo rappel from a towering arch, cross a raging river, descend into a dark cave and face off against a snake; Scott details growing up with his famous father.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, James Marsden
programme.desc: X-man' James Marsden joins Bear on an adventure into the heart Rattle Snake Canyon in Utah, where they helicopter on to one of the highest arches in the area.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Don Cheadle
programme.desc: Actor Don Cheadle joins Bear on a trek through the mountains of New England, which includes crossing a raging river and a climb to a towering railroad bridge. Bear considers a porcupine carcass for dinner.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Drew Brees
programme.desc: Bear Grylls ventures deep into Panama's Camino de Cruces jungle with Super Bowl winning quarterback Drew Brees, exploring caves and wrestling a six-foot crocodile.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Shaquille O'Neal
programme.desc: Bear and NBA legend Shaquille O'Neal are lifted from a freight train via helicopter and whisked away to a dense forest in the remote Adirondacks; they face blood-sucking leeches, and a 200-foot rock face as they search for their exit.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Marshawn Lynch
programme.desc: Bear and Super Bowl champion Marshawn Lynch tackle a rugged mountain range that has sharp cliffs and dark chasms; the pair comes face to face with the French Foreign Legion and pursues a wild hog for dinner.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Roger Federer
programme.desc: Bear takes one of the best athletes of all time, Roger Federer, on a daring adventure through the Swiss Alps as they attempt to navigate the tough terrain. They descend a frozen waterfall and use tennis rackets as snowshoes.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: 7 Celebrities,7 Days, 7 Craziest Challenges with Bear Grylls starting: Zac Efron
programme.desc: Hollywood actor Zac Efron heads into the Appalachian wilderness of the Catskill Mountains in New York. They parachute into the woods, rappel down cliffs and search for shelter.
programme.sub-title: 7 Celebrities,7 Days, 7 Craziest Challenges with Bear Grylls starting: Zac Efron

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Lena Headey
programme.desc: Lena Headey and Bear journey through Spain's Iberian Peninsula; they rappel from a towering ridge, dine on bone marrow from a goat carcass and cross a lake using a makeshift raft during an unexpected storm; Lena details her life and rise to stardom.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: 7 Celebrities,7 Days, 7 Craziest Challenges with Bear Grylls starting: Courtney Cox
programme.desc: Actress Courteney Cox joins Bear to challenge herself into sharp edged discomfort amidst the jagged peaks and descending cliffs on the windswept coast of Ireland.
programme.sub-title: 7 Celebrities,7 Days, 7 Craziest Challenges with Bear Grylls starting: Courtney Cox

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Derek Hough
programme.desc: Dancer Derek Hough joins Bear on an adventure across the mountains of Bulgaria; Derek reveals childhood struggles and teaches Bear some dance moves.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Lindsey Vonn
programme.desc: Bear and Olympic gold medallist Lindsey Vonn take a speedboat trip, then swim to shore and make their way along a rugged coastline; after traversing a deep chasm, they collect a dinner of sea urchins.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Kate Hudson
programme.desc: Oscar-nominated actress Kate Hudson goes on a journey with Bear that involves a treacherous mountain range in Italy; Kate shares details of her unique upbringing.

programme.title: Running Wild With Bear Grylls: Bear Grylls, Sterling K. Brown
programme.desc: Actor Sterling K. Brown joins Bear in the high mountains of Colorado, where they battle plummeting temperatures while descending a sheer rock face, track an elusive deer and rappel down a waterfall.

programme.title: JAG: Cowboys and Cossaks
programme.desc: Harm, Mac, and Bud are assigned sea duty to monitor a joint war games exercise between U.S. and Russian naval forces.

programme.title: JAG: The Guardian
programme.desc: Harm and Mac defend Bauwer, a former Navy Seal, now a homeless man, who's accused of using excessive force when he killed three men who tried to hold up a convenience store.

programme.title: JAG: Rendevous
programme.desc: Mac and Harm once again find themselves courtroom adversaries as Mac is assigned the difficult task of defending CPO Holst, an admitted wife abuser accused of murdering his wife's boyfriend.

programme.title: JAG: Ghost Ship
programme.desc: Harm and Mac investigate the discovery of a skeleton found hidden aboard a decommissioned aircraft carrier that is being cut up for scrap, and learn it is all that remains of a long-missing JAG officer.

programme.title: JAG: The Court Martial of Sandra Gilbert
programme.desc: The top female gunship pilot at the Yuma Marine Base is charged with disobeying an order to stop "fraternizing" with her crew chief, a married, enlisted man. Mac is assigned as prosecutor and Harm is set as Susan's defense attorney.
programme.sub-title: The Court Martial of Sandra Gilbert

programme.title: JAG: Vanished
programme.desc: Concerns regarding the mysterious disappearance of a fully armed Navy F-14 during a storm lead Harm's investigation to consider such diverse scenarios as pilot suicide, terrorism, the Bermuda Triangle curse, or UFO interference.

programme.title: JAG: King of the Fleas
programme.desc: Having just stabbed a man on a street-corner, Willie, a wheelchair-bound former Vietnam POW arrives at JAG headquarters to report "a 30-year-old murder" which he's just committed. Willie relates that while imprisoned he knew Harm's father.

programme.title: JAG: Against All Enemies
programme.desc: Harm, Mac and Bud board an aircraft carrier in the Sea of Japan to investigate the accidental shooting down of a North Korean civilian airliner by Navy jets.

programme.title: JAG: Impact
programme.desc: Harm and Bud investigate the crash of a Marine helicopter whose dead pilot is being blamed for the incident. They discover the chopper seems to have been hit by some form of UFO. Government security officials want to maintain a cover-up at all costs.

programme.title: JAG: People V. Rabb
programme.desc: Harm is arrested by the FBI for the murder of Konaplanik, a Russian who was selling Harm supposedly accurate information about his missing father's whereabouts. The killer turns out to be Col. Tomashevsky, a Russian agent.

programme.title: JAG: Defensless
programme.desc: A woman Navy ensign shoots and kills a Turkish colonel; she claims self-defense.

programme.title: JAG: With Intent to Die
programme.desc: With help from Harm, Mac, Harriet, and Bud, Admiral Chedwiggen investigates the apparent suicide of his mentor, Admiral Clancy, who allegedly shot himself while on a hunting trip. Harm and company ultimately uncover who the killer was.

programme.title: JAG: Father's Day
programme.desc: Mac and Harm are assigned the task of defending a young Marine whose frustration at being unable to wrest custody of his son has led to his being court-martialed for dereliction of duty.

programme.title: JAG: Tiger, Tiger
programme.desc: Harm takes Josh on a weekend cruise for Navy families aboard a frigate. When the frigate is taken over by terrorists who intend to fire missiles at Cuba, Harm and Bud take matters into their own hands to stop the evil plot.

programme.title: JAG: Return of Mimmy Blackhorse
programme.desc: An Army forensic pathologist joins forces with Harm, Mac, Bud and Harriet as they try to verify that newly unearthed human remains are those of a heroic Navajo "code-talker" who had been missing since WWII.

programme.title: JAG: The Imposter
programme.desc: Harm is held captive by Palmer, a renegade government agent with whom he had crossed swords a few months ago. While Palmer prepares a life mask so he can pass for Harm, he explains his plans to assassinate the key witness in the trial.

programme.title: JAG: To Russia With Love
programme.desc: With evidence that his missing father could still be alive in, Harm convinces the Admiral to give him time off so he can go to Russia. In Moscow, they are questioned by Mark Falcon, the KGB agent who had stolen a list of POW's earlier from Harm.

programme.title: JAG: Gypsy Eyes
programme.desc: Unable to lose the pursuing Russian fighters, Harm's and Mac’s hijacked Mig is shot out of the sky over Siberia.

programme.title: JAG: Act of Terror
programme.desc: When Arab terrorists attach a mine to a US Navy warship in an attempt to sink it, Marine guards are able to kill one and capture the other, even after the mine explodes, killing two sailors.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A man gets busted during a drug deal, a cyclist gets confused when an officer finds narcotics in his pants, and officers respond to reports of gunfire.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A daring driver takes officers on a wild ride, a man offers a bizarre excuse for the items found in his car, and a man is arrested after threatening his neighbor with a gun.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Officers suspect meth use by a man sleeping in a bus station, a pedestrian's multiple bags of drugs suggest trafficking, and a drunk driver claims she was set up.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A young woman claims not to know her passengers, the search of a man's car leads to more charges, and a woman learns not to start vehicles with a screwdriver.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A wanted woman gives deputies a fake name, but her brother accidentally reveals the truth, and two officers respond to a domestic dispute.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A wanted man sprints across a freeway to evade police, a woman is arrested for breaking her friend's windows, and a driver tries to talk his way out of a road rage arrest.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A sluggish suspect attempts to outrun the police, officers try to piece together a wild fight between neighbors, and a man's plot to extort money from an ex goes awry.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Police go off-roading to follow a stolen car, a man has odd excuses for having suspicious items, and an officer settles a dispute between a wife and her jealous husband.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A fleeing motorcyclist crashes into an occupied trailer, a stubborn suspect plays dumb, and officers are disappointed when they run into a repeat offender.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Fort Bend, Texas deputies arrest a man for possession of a crack pipe, and Michigan and Washington police both respond to domestic violence situations.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A fleeing vehicle leads an officer on a hot pursuit, a deputy encounters a nervous driver with a big secret, and a young cyclist tries to make a quick getaway.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Officers split up to interrogate two men suspected of stealing a pick-up truck, a motorist tries to ditch a bag of meth, and police discover a bunch of syringes in a vehicle.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A traffic stop takes a surprising turn when a suspect creates a spur-of-the-moment diversion, and a soft-spoken driver has difficulty being upfront with the officers.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A convenience store sprinter bets officers he'll get away next time, matching bullets help solve an armed robbery, and a trucker takes his family on a supersized car chase.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A cyclist leads officers on a low-speed chase, a motorist who led police on a dangerous chase feigns ignorance, and a couple's date ends with a totaled car.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A quiet passenger makes a run for it during a traffic stop, a stolen puppy is safely returned to its family, and a struggling woman hits a speed bump on her road to recovery.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A man on a motorcycle evades an officer, a woman in a suspicious vehicle accrues multiple charges, and a traffic stop reveals two men under the influence.

programme.desc: A reality show on real alcohol and drug testing performed on Australian roads by the Police's RBT Squad.

programme.desc: A brilliant plain-Jane (Shelley Hack) becomes a fetching glamour gal with the help of Julie (Lauren Tewes), and falls in love with a man (Kevin Tighe) who has some mistaken ideas about her.
programme.sub-title: Dumb Luck / Tres Amigos / Hey, Jealous Lover

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Invisible Maniac / September Song / Peekaboo
programme.desc: Isaac (Ted Lange) finds himself in a precarious position when he meets a beautiful former school chum (Bernadette Stanis) and her macho husband (Clifton Davis).
programme.sub-title: The Invisible Maniac / September Song / Peekaboo

programme.title: The Love Boat: Marriage-A-Thon Cruise / The Promoter - Part 1
programme.desc: Romance and comic intrigue reign on the high seas as 25 engaged couples board the Pacific Princess to compete in a "Marriage-A-Thon Cruise." (Part 1)

programme.title: The Love Boat: Marriage-A-Thon Cruise / The Promoter - Part 2
programme.desc: Darren McGavin, as dynamic promoter Larry Evans, recruits the services of "The Love Boat" to take the brides-and-grooms-to-be, who are competing for a house, a car, and $50,000 in cash, on a cruise that will wind up in a mass wedding in Acapulco. (Part 2)

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Mallory Quest / The Offer / Julie, The Vamp - Part 1

programme.sub-title: The Mallory Quest / The Offer / Julie, The Vamp - Part 1

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Mallory Quest / The Offer / Julie, The Vamp - Part 2

programme.sub-title: The Mallory Quest / The Offer / Julie, The Vamp - Part 2

programme.sub-title: Tell Her She's Great / Matchmaker, Matchmaker Times Two / The Baby Alarm

programme.title: Melrose Place: A Melrose Place Christmas
programme.desc: Tis the season for surprises when Alison comes out of surgery with a romantic revelation and Michael makes his first baby delivery.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Single White Sister
programme.desc: Jake has accompanied Jo to the beach for a very early morning session picture- taking. They find an increasing attraction developing between them. (TV-PG)

programme.title: Melrose Place: Peanut Butter And Jealousy
programme.desc: Some relationships aren't made to last as Jo considers going back to her old marriage and Jane bursts at the seams over her sister's overly friendly behavior.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Picture Imperfect
programme.desc: Advertising gets a whole lot sexier when Alison gets an unexpected job promotion, Amanda Woodward comes on board as her boss, and Jo takes some very revealing shots for an underwear ad.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Three's A Crowd
programme.desc: Things heat up fast when Jake and Jo move their relationship to the next level, and Amanda is immediately attracted to Alison's roommate, Billy, after a game of Truth or Dare.

programme.title: Melrose Place: My New Partner
programme.desc: There's a little too much of mixing business and pleasure when Alison confronts Billy about his dates with Amanda, and Jo has an interesting solution to Jake's unemployment woes.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Bye Bye Billy
programme.desc: Alison is terrified when she's accosted after a theater performance, and her decision to find safety in Keith's arms has Billy packing his bags.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Irreconcilable Similarities
programme.desc: As both of their relationships take the fast track to commitment and compromise, Alison and Billy both begin to wonder if they're making the right move.

programme.title: Melrose Place: End Game
programme.desc: Billy is shaken by the sudden death of his father but is even more surprised when both Amanda and Alison show up at the funeral.

programme.title: Melrose Place: The Test
programme.desc: It's a wicked game of "cat and mouse" when Alison begs Amanda for her old advertising job and Billy finds his padded resume gets him in more trouble than it's worth.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Pushing Boundaries
programme.desc: Things heat up at the workplace as Kimberly reveals to Michael that she wants to be more than just friends, and Billy finds himself doing too much "overtime" with his new boss, Nancy.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Pas de Trois
programme.desc: Money makes everything messy when Jo and Jake have a falling-out over taxes, Jane's new fashion assignment has her spending less and less time with Michael, and Amanda flaunts her higher position in the company.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Carpe Diem
programme.desc: It's a romantic evening to remember when Alison and Billy have their first "date" and Michael finds himself torn between two women on the night of their wedding anniversary.

programme.title: Melrose Place: State of Need
programme.desc: There are no easy answers when Amanda announces she's pregnant with Billy's child and Jane agrees to let Michael sleep on the couch if they go to couple's therapy.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Suspicious Minds
programme.desc: Melrose Place is for sale, and while Jane and Michael wonder if a new start might help their struggling relationship, Alison is afraid to leave her apartment after receiving threatening phone calls.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Much Ado About Everything
programme.desc: Amanda throws herself a housewarming party but forces Alison to work late so that she can hit on Billy.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Long Night's Journey
programme.desc: Billy remains wary of Alison's relationship with Keith. Alison agrees to drive Keith to the airport.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Revenge
programme.desc: Billy shows up at Keith's worksite and assaults him. After Alison tells Keith that she hates him, he commits suicide while on the phone with her.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Fire Power
programme.desc: Amanda threatens to fire Alison when her drinking gets out of hand. Billy forces her to confront her guilt over Keith's suicide.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: What Price Harvey?
programme.desc: Magical ambition shows its dark side when Harvey drops out of school to become an industrial tycoon.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina And The Pirates

programme.desc: When typed on a magical typewriter, Sabrina’s spy story and its characters come to life.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Salem The Boy

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina’s Pen Pal
programme.desc: Sabrina discovers her new pen pal witch is wanted in two realms.

programme.desc: Her Witches License and a new student bring Sabrina a new set of challenges.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Salem And Juliette
programme.desc: Sabrina helps Salem find a date for his high school reunion.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Jealousy

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Prelude To A Kiss

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Phantom Menace

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Little Orphan Hilda

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Spoiled Rotten
programme.desc: New charge card from the Other Realm causes Sabrina to forget that the most important things in life can’t be bought.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Aging Not So Gracefully

programme.sub-title: Love Means Having To Say You’re Sorry

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Ice Station Sabrina
programme.desc: A life-threatening situation forces Sabrina to risk exposing her magic to a witch hunter.

programme.desc: When the weather outside turns frightful, the Spellman’s plan a trip to Jamaica.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Super Hero

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Salem’s Daughter

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Love In Bloom
programme.desc: Valentine’s Day gets complicated when Sabrina learns two boys are vying for her heart.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Welcome Back Duke

programme.desc: Cousin Amanda returns to give Sabrina a little competition in the teenage witch -and love-- department.

programme.desc: The West gets even wilder when Sabrina takes the reins as town sheriff.

programme.desc: Sabrina’s Sweet 16 becomes a truly magical birthday when she discovers she’s a witch.

programme.title: American Ninja Warrior

programme.title: American Ninja Warrior

programme.title: American Ninja Warrior

programme.title: American Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: The finals return to Las Vegas where the ninjas face up to eight super-sized obstacles in Stage 1. The ninjas must complete the course, including new obstacles Three Ring Circus and Tetherball, to advance to Stage 2 of the national finals.

programme.title: American Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: The finals continue in Las Vegas where the ninjas face super-sized obstacles in Stages 1 and 2 in timed runs; the ninjas who advance to Stage 2 must complete a six-obstacle course, including new obstacle Hornet's Nest, to advance to Stage 3.

programme.title: American Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: The national finals conclude in Las Vegas as the remaining competitors move on to stages 3 and 4 for their chance to win $1 million and become the next American Ninja Warrior.

programme.desc: Elmo learns about how different kinds of bells are used for different purposes, while Mr Noodle has trouble figuring out how to use a bell.

programme.desc: Mr Noodle has trouble trying to ride a seesaw, and Elmo counts how many times a hippo can go up and down on a pogo stick.

programme.title: Elmo's World
programme.desc: Elmo learns about how different kinds of bells are used for different purposes, while Mr Noodle has trouble figuring out how to use a bell.

programme.desc: Elmo and friends learn about dinosaurs, so Mr Noodle acts like a dinosaur, and Elmo counts how many baby dinosaurs hatch from dinosaur eggs.

programme.desc: Elmo learns about different kinds of cameras and how to take a picture. Mr Noodle tries to take a picture of a talking cheese, and Elmo counts the pictures in his photo album.

programme.desc: Elmo learns about jumping and counts some jumping beans while Mr Noodle tries to figure out how to jump over a hurdle.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas has to take the new track inspection vehicle out on all his jobs today.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: A photograph of the engines is to be taken for a big billboard, but all hell breaks loose when Diesel accidentally rolls in front of the camera and obscures Thomas.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Edward is chosen to deliver a new brass water wheel to the water works. However, there is trouble ahead when the proud engine decides to take the scenic route in order to show his friends his valuable load.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: It is carnival time on Sodor and Rosie is hoping to pull the special coaches, but when the job is given to Emily, Rosie decides to show her worth by attempting to shift the funfair herself.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Miss Marvel shares her tale of Proteus the mystical engine.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: When the Sodor Wishing Tree is damaged in a storm, Henry makes every effort to prevent some woodsmen from reaching it and chopping it down. However, little does he know that the woodsmen have actually come to repair the tree.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: When a big, brash engine called Hank offers to help Thomas with his chores, the little engine shrugs off his assistance, but Thomas comes to regret being so hasty when he cracks a cylinder and needs some help.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Toby is determined to find something special on the island in order to impress Sir Topham Hatt, but after searching all over Sodor without success, he runs out of coal and must call for help.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Emily gets too big for her boots after some praise from the Fat Controller.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Freddie decides to organise a winter party at the wharf instead of at Mr Percival's house so that Colin the Crane can join in. However, the forgetful engine neglects to tell Mr Percival and all of the equipment is loaded onto a barge.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: When a Sodor fireman is to be presented with a medal for bravery, Thomas decides to be a hero too. He tries to prove himself but causes havoc and disaster wherever he goes. Can he make amends?

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Duncan feels hurt when, instead of a train ride, Mr Percival decides to take the twins for a birthday trip in a hot-air balloon.
programme.sub-title: Duncan and the Hot Air Balloon

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: James ignores Hector the truck's advice and sets off in the middle of some bad winter weather. When he gets stuck in a snow drift, James soon wishes he had listened to Hector.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Toby is given the job of leading the parade at the first Great Waterton festival with a new tram called Flora.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Diesel challenges Thomas to a race backwards all around Sodor. Unable to see where they are going, the two engines cause chaos everywhere.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Gordon is a very proud engine and thinks he knows the railway better than anyone else. When he takes a shortcut to the docks, he gets lost and refuses to ask for directions.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: It is Mr Percival's birthday and Sir Handel is going to tell the story of the `Man in the Hills'. While picking up a load of presents, Thomas finds a mysterious shape in the side of a hill and decides to reorganise Mr Percival's party.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas has been given the job of delivering specially made blocks for the Sodor River Bridge, but things go awry when his brakes begin to fail.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Percy is charged with taking Lady Hatt to her surprise party at the new bandstand. However, little realising the purpose of her journey, Lady Hatt instructs the engine to take her elsewhere.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: The puppet show has arrived on Sodor and Skarloey is determined to pull the special train. However, his refusal to accept help with the heavy trucks causes chaos across the railway.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas and Percy have a falling out when the former decides to keep a secret from his pal.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: It is the day of the spring party at the Duke and Duchess of Boxford's summer house. As the engines make the necessary preparations, Cranky teases Thomas, saying he can only carry light loads.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas is given the job of transporting a very special statue. However, he thinks his cargo is a real lion and attempts to feed it all kinds of food.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Whilst covered in a pink undercoat, James is asked to pick up the Fat Controller's granddaughter, but the proud engine is worried that his friends will tease him.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas is sent to pick up the Fat Controller to take him to a birthday party. However, when he arrives at the station, the Fat Controller seems to have been replaced by a mischievous impostor.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas discovers he has something of a skill for comedy when an assignment to collect a cargo of bubbles goes awry.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: While Percy is at the steam works being repaired, Thomas is excited to be given the job of doing the mail run for him. Gordon advises the cheeky engine to seek Percy's advice, but Thomas thinks he knows it all.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: When famous opera singer Alicia Botti arrives, Thomas is bubbling with pride as he is sent to take her to a grand concert. New engine Charlie also makes his debut, will he turn out to be even more fun than Thomas?

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Farmer Trotter asks Thomas to collect some straw for the piglets, which are soon to be born. On the way, Thomas sees other things he thinks the piglets will like.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: The Fat Controller announces that the library is holding a story time for the children and that he needs an engine to deliver the storybooks.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Percy is asked to deliver Dowager Hatt's birthday present from the Fat Controller. Percy is so proud that he shows off his gift crate to Mavis - but at the quarry it gets covered in slate dust.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Toby is fitted with a brand new three-chime whistle while his little bell is repaired. He finds his new device a bit daunting, and so comes up with a plan.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: When Mavis does not say hello, Emily assumes she is sad and chuffs to the quarry to cheer her up with some of the new flowers - but disaster strikes.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: When Mavis does not say hello, Emily assumes she is sad and chuffs to the quarry to cheer her up with some of the new flowers - but disaster strikes.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: The engines are preparing for the Sodor kite gala. Meanwhile, the Fat Controller's grandchildren are up on Gordon's Hill with their multicoloured kite.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Victor has to leave the steam works, with Thomas taking over for the day. Victor tries to tell Thomas how to run the works in his absence, but Thomas doesn't listen.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: It's a rainy day on Sodor, with big puddles everywhere. Thomas decides to have a game of splashing puddles with Rosie. It is so much fun that he forgets to pick up the Fat Controller and Alicia Botti.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Hiro returns to the island. The Fat Controller tells Thomas and Percy that he is organising a welcome party at Knapford, but Thomas forgets to tell the other engines.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Gordon decides he is the strongest engine. Ignoring the advice of the Fat Controller not to climb any steep hills in the snow, Gordon crashes into some trucks and derails.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Henry wants to do something helpful, but instead he lets a herd of pigs get dirty in a field and frightens away the Sodor Warbler! Can Henry redeem himself?

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas is excited when he is asked to deliver the new beehives to Farmer Trotter. However, when he takes a route by a flowery field, the bees buzz away and refuse to come back.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: When Hiro sees that the Fat Controller is very busy, he decides to help out by making some decisions for him, but is Hiro being as helpful as he thinks he is?

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: All kinds of shenanigans ensue when Thomas is called upon to deliver a giraffe to the Sodor Wildlife Centre.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: All of the engines are in a state of excitement as the winter holidays arrive. Most excited of all is Henry, who must deliver a special box to a field. Henry wonders what the package contains.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: James refuses to be fitted with a lamp to help him see in the dark; he regrets his decision when he picks up the wrong person.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: On the day of a special Sodor football tournament, all the engines are given important jobs - except Emily, who is tasked with picking up some dirty washing.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Edward tries to show his friend Charlie that he is a really fun engine, to the detriment of his job; deciding to be called Eddie, Edward spends the day larking around, but things go somewhat awry.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Toby must visit the summerhouse, which means travelling through the scary Whistling Woods. The little engine is determined to get through the woods without being frightened, until all kinds of noises send him steaming off in the opposite direction.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Whilst chuffing down the line, Henry collides with a truck and cracks his right siderod. Hiro pushes him to the Steamworks where Victor tells Henry all about health and safety. Henry takes the message to heart, and causes chaos.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Diesel swaps his dull load of slate for a haul of shiny red apples in order to please Sodor's school children.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas is amused by the popping sounds coming from his fizzy load of lemonade, but when he delivers the cargo to a children's party, he discovers that the bottles are empty because of his antics. Can Thomas save the day?

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Victor bites off more than he can chew when he agrees to fix almost every engine in Sodor on the same day. Will Victor learn that it is okay to say no sometimes?

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: The Island Inspector pays a visit to Sodor, but Thomas's ambitious plans to impress him lead to chaos - and flying coal trucks.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Percy decides to imitate wise old Gordon in order to win the respect of the other engines. However, Percy's plan backfires when his new project means that he forgets to deliver his mail.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas is given the very important job of delivering the Star of Knapford. Instead of going directly to Knapford station, he decides to grant all his friends a wish upon the star.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas is anxious to help the children find some hats for their snowmen, so he decides to borrow some from the people of Sodor without asking permission first.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas tries to be in two places at once. The Fat Controller enlists Thomas to teach the silly Misty Island Logging Locos, but he has already promised to play a game of hide-and-seek with Percy.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas is ordered to collect some precious Jobi wood from Misty Island. Chaos ensues when naughty crane Old Wheezy starts throwing the logs everywhere.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas decides that it is high time new engine Scruff took a bath. However, when the grubby newcomer gets wind of Thomas's plan, he flees in terror.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Gordon is put in charge of Whiff's Waste Dump for the day, deciding to hide whenever one of his friends appears at the dump. As a result, the waste begins to pile up and Gordon's domain gets smellier and smellier.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas is given the job of escorting the Fat Controller's mother, Dowager Hatt, on a tour of Sodor. However, she is less than impressed by her guide's choice of important sights.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Dash, Bash and Ferdinand decide to throw a Christmas party. Before long, the logging locos have caused chaos on Misty Island and it is down to Thomas to save the day.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: The Fat Controller gives Ferdinand the honour of helping Gordon take the Lion of Sodor to the summerhouse. Gordon, however, gets very jealous, which causes a nasty accident on the track.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Dash is rewarded by the Fat Controller for being a `Really Useful Engine'. His prize is to spend the day with Toby. Dash enjoys himself so much that Toby thinks his friend would like to live on Sodor.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Dash shows Emily how to make special tweeting noises with his whistle, but he does it so often he runs out of fuel and gets stuck. Then Emily thoughtlessly goes onto the fair without him.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Percy wants to make friends with the wild animals on Sodor, but they are frightened of him and run away. Percy realises that if he is gentle and quiet rather than fast and loud, he will stop scaring them.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Edward is given a special job to do, so he decides that he needs to become a hero. He soon discovers that heroes come in all shapes and sizes.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: James shuns Toby's help and refuses to work as part of a team when Gordon needs rescuing. However, James soon has to swallow his pride and ask Toby to assist him.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas tries to cheer Hiro up when he sees that he does not look very happy. Hiro tells Thomas he is sad because he is missing home, so Thomas takes him on a trip to forget his woes.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: While Thomas and Percy are delivering a giant balloon, the wind on the coast track whips it up into the sky. Gordon explains that it will not come back, so the engines have to find another way of replacing it.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Henry is given a special task of taking a very important visitor around on a tour, but finds himself teased about the special coal he has to use.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Gordon and Thomas collect logs for an animal shelter and sing a song about snow in the hope that they can make snow clouds appear.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas is excited because it is time for a winter party. However, Thomas's friends need help to be fixed so they will not be able to make it. Thomas tries to bring the party to them - with chaotic results.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: It is a very foggy day and the engines are asked to stay put until the weather clears. However, Spencer and Percy do not heed the warning and get lost in the fog.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas gives Bertie a tour, but then Bertie realises he will be late to collect a group of people.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas meets Butch, who has a magnet attached to his crane arm, which impresses Thomas greatly. However, he and Butch do not listen to Victor's guidance on how to use the magnet and havoc soon ensues.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Advance On the Mystery Planet
programme.desc: Believing that all the jungles of Earth are inhabited by the Mazone, Harlock decides to return to the Pirate Island.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: When Lola Shines Golden
programme.desc: The Arcadia successfully makes its way to Venus.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Mother, Be Eternal
programme.desc: Tadashi is upset at himself for talking about the Arcadia to Lola.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Castle of Evil in the Sea of Death
programme.desc: The Arcadia heads back to Earth to investigate a third signal being emitted from the Sargasso Sea.
programme.sub-title: The Castle of Evil in the Sea of Death

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Mausoleum of the Sphinx
programme.desc: Commander Kiruta takes Mayu with him. Desperate to find her, Harlock receives a message from Commander Kiruta.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Tragic Love! the Northern Pole Aurora
programme.desc: The Arcadia is at the north pole after spotting an aurora that closely resembles a runway.
programme.sub-title: Tragic Love! the Northern Pole Aurora

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Kei - A Farewell Song
programme.desc: The operator, Kei Yuki, goes to visit her parents' grave.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Skeleton Hero
programme.desc: Upon finding a message in a bottle floating in space that contains an SOS message from Captain Yamanaka of the Braves.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Evil Shadow Soldiers
programme.desc: A group of Mazone suddenly appear in the ship.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Queen Lafresias Trap
programme.desc: There are supposed to be forty-two crewman aboard the Arcadia.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Extinct Planet Jura
programme.desc: Miime's old planet is named Jura, and it was once devastated by a nuclear attack.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Gohrum! the Tragic Soldier
programme.desc: An asteroid belt hits the Arcadia.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Space Graveyard: Deathshadow
programme.desc: Upon returning to Deathshadow Island with the memory device left behind by Zol, Harlock and his crew learn something huge about Queen Lafresia's flagship.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Yattaran, Song of a Plastic Model Fanatic
programme.desc: Upon victory, Harlock orders First mate Yattaran to secure the hostages.
programme.sub-title: Yattaran, Song of a Plastic Model Fanatic

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Shooting Star of Pure Love
programme.desc: A surprising couple, Zorba, the son of the warrior Zol, and a Mazone medic named Lucia, escaped from the Mazone fleet.
programme.sub-title: The Shooting Star of Pure Love

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Dr. Zero and Mii
programme.desc: Doctor Zero becomes extremely resentful over Zorba and Lucia's murder.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: A Long Way Away Voyage
programme.desc: Due to Harlock's unrelenting resistance, there is a growing resentment between the Mazone military and its civilians.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Will of the Arcadia
programme.desc: Having a hunch as to the reason why Harlock so staunchly opposes them, Queen Lafresia sends someone on a mission to Earth to find out why.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Ulysses Nebula
programme.desc: In order to rescue Mayu, Harlock allows the will of the ship to choose its own course.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Life-and-Death Struggle On the Rainbow Planet
programme.desc: The Mazone come attacking the Arcadia which has had too much free time lately.
programme.sub-title: The Life-and-Death Struggle On the Rainbow Planet

programme.title: Captain Harlock: My Friend, My Youth
programme.desc: While the Arcadia is being repaired at the dock of a Pirate Island, Tadashi and Tori-san come back with a pair of glasses.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Arcadia: The Secret Story of Its Construction
programme.desc: The Arcadia is rapidly being repaired on a Pirate Island while Lafresia continues her bloodthirsty hunt for it.
programme.sub-title: Arcadia: The Secret Story of Its Construction

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Call of the Star Flute
programme.desc: The Mazone plot to use Mayu to lure Harlock and the Arcadia to the Wind Planet and then attack with a powerful mine.
programme.sub-title: The Call of the Star Flute

programme.title: Captain Harlock: A Lone Man's Charge
programme.desc: Commander Kiruta learns of the existence of the Mazone and persistently tries to inform the Prime Minister of the impending doom.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: A Galactic Lullaby
programme.desc: The Arcadia is trapped due to an artificial gravity device.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Beautiful Mysterious Woman
programme.desc: After rescuing Mayu, all that remains is the final showdown with Lafresia.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: The Eve of the Showdown
programme.desc: The beautiful, dazzling Shizuka Namino becomes a bit of a celebrity on board the Arcadia after being rescued.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Tears On a Red Sweater
programme.desc: Upon realizing that Harlock knew who Shizuka's real identity is, she deems that she has failed in her mission.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Farewell! Mayu
programme.desc: The invasion Mazone forces finally begin their attack on Earth.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Heroic! The Commander Dies
programme.desc: The Arcadia leaves for space with Kiruta on board, after he was injured while trying to save Mayu.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Then, The Angels Sang
programme.desc: After a bunch of skirmishes, the true battle finally begins.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Duel! The Queen VS. Harlock
programme.desc: Making its way past countless gunshots, the Arcadia finally arrives at Queen Lafresia's flagship.

programme.title: Captain Harlock: Farewell, Space Outlaw
programme.desc: Harlock and his crew go for their triumphant return to Earth, but, all that awaits them is an order that they've been banished from Earth.

programme.title: Galaxy Express 999: The Red Winds of Mars
programme.desc: The capital of Mars, Great Syrtis. It is a deserted planet with nothing but the blowing of the red winds.

programme.title: Galaxy Express 999: Titan's Sleeping Warrior
programme.desc: Tetsuro is rescued from a ghost by Claire, a waitress they met. But in doing so, Claire is blasted into a million pieces.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Graham's race through the desert takes a sudden turn from make to break.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Kurt loses his way hauling a hut across Australia.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Bumper Farrell is on a mercy dash, leading a hay convoy into the eye of the storm.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: A team of heavyweight champions drag a million dollar shed through the eye of a needle.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Mark Cromwell and the electricity switch room convoy run into freak weather.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: It's trial by fire for Russell McDonough, hauling a mobile building down a tricky track.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The Mackay Brothers hit the bush and try to dodge destruction.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Nigel Vagg hauls 900 feral goats through a heatwave.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Sludge and the Phantom ride into battle fighting the elements.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Sludge takes on foreign roads and strange loads in the land of the kiwi.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Sludge experiences a white-knuckle ride, climbing a mountain with an angry load.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Steve Grahame hits the mud, and Sludge's mountain mission takes a downhill slide.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Husband and wife team Nick and Jo face tough driving conditions with volatile cargo.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Russel tackles a graveyard of wrecked cars.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: While preparing for a gruelling trip, Steve faces a malfunctioning clutch.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Breakdowns and engine problems push Steve behind schedule in the Outback.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Jeff is confronted with a heavy storm while on the road in Tasmania.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Jeff faces a treacherous pass in wet conditions.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Anthony and Danielle have an urgent water-drilling job to replace a collapsed well.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Lacey's free Pilates classes are met with hostility in Dog River.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brent babysits Wanda's son to impress Lacey, but chaos ensues.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Karen tries to solve the mystery of the "Grad 68" graffiti. Brent's new bathroom signs cause chaos.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Dog River seeks a unique tourist attraction, while Oscar fends off an admirer.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: The town's elaborate plan to shield Lacey from her ex-fiancé goes awry.
programme.sub-title: All My Exes Live in Toronto

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brent's successful cousin returns, sparking jealousy and a surprising talent show.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brent's new cell phone ignites a tech rivalry with Davis and a historical preservation effort.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brent feuds with a comedian, while Hank's heckling skills go too far.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brent's slogans cause conflict. Oscar's memory loss worries Brent and Emma.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brent considers an offer from a rival hockey team after feeling taken for granted.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: The Dog River gang's Grey Cup trip is filled with mishaps and a revelation for Brent.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Lacey and Brent figure out their relationship. Emma discovers her savings are gone.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brent finds out his parents never married. Hank disputes Brent's ownership of a card.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Davis regains his sense of smell. Brent realizes he knows little about Dog River.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Lacey's birthday party is ruined by her deepest fear. Brent and Hank try to find Wanda's fear.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Hank needs help with work at his cousin's farm. Brent ensures he doesn't owe Hank any favors.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brent buys a big screen TV and becomes an object of pity. Lacey buys jewelry from Wanda.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: The town is in an uproar over getting a traffic light installed. Lacey makes the Ruby Club sandwich.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brent installs a security camera at Corner Gas. Hank fulfills a lifelong dream.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Wanda steps in as the bingo caller. Karen hangs out with Brent.

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Mosquitoes hit the town hard. Lacey participates in a Dog River activity. Oscar returns to Corner Gas.

programme.desc: There's curling drama in Dog River when Brent and Oscar end up skipping separate teams.

programme.desc: Hank becomes goodwill ambassador and shows an American around town.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Grandpa Campbell
programme.desc: When Ray's ne'er-do-well father comes for a visit, he turns the household upside-down -- and turns Tamera into a ruthless businesswoman!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Twins Get Fired
programme.desc: When Tia becomes Tamera's new boss at Rocket Burger, they both learn a lesson in responsibility

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Halloween
programme.desc: While the twins go in search of a Halloween rave, Lisa and Ray attend a fancy party thrown by a prospective client.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Weird Science
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera think they're in for a "free ride" when Ray starts dating their science teacher.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Thanksgiving in Hawaii (Part I)
programme.desc: When Ray and Lisa take the girls to Hawaii for Thanksgiving, they're forced to share their condo with their "exes," who have begun dating each other.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Thanksgiving in Hawaii (Part II)
programme.desc: While Ray and Lisa frantically search for the twins, who are missing in Hawaii, Roger opens the Campbell/Landry home to hobos for Thanksgiving dinner.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Private School
programme.desc: The prospect of Tia's attending a prestigious prep school puts social pressure on Tia and financial pressure on Lisa.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Christmas
programme.desc: When a loan shark comes after Ray's father for a bad debt, the family comes to his rescue.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Double Date
programme.desc: The twins find themselves in double trouble when they switch identities and Tia winds up with two dates for the same party.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Reality Really Bites
programme.desc: For her film class, Tia "spices up" her family documentary by throwing all sorts of monkey wrenches into their lives.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Volunteers
programme.desc: While volunteering at a local retirement home, Tamera becomes determined to help a grumpy old man find happiness again.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Valentines Day
programme.desc: Valentine's Day finds Tamera trying to "steal back" her hot date, and Ray and Lisa wondering if the other person is in love with them.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Paper or Plastic?
programme.desc: Tamera crosses the picket lines to work at a supermarket, but soon realizes that being a "scab" isn't all it's cracked up to be.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Piano Lesson
programme.desc: Ray learns a "noteworthy" lesson in honesty when he "fibs" and tells a tone-deaf Tamera that she plays the piano well, and she winds up humiliating herself at a piano recital.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Summer Bummer
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera trade places so that Tia can be with a cute guy in summer school and Tamera can "goof off" by taking Tia's spot as a camp counselor.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Candidate
programme.desc: Tamera learns how much she needs Tia's help when she runs for class president.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Big Twin on Campus
programme.desc: When Tia poses as a college student to impress her older boyfriend, Tamera goes to great lengths to fit in with Tia's new circle of "mature" friends.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Audition
programme.desc: Tamera's budding career as a lowdown blues singer is threatened when she gets "too happy" after her dream date asks her out!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: When a Man Loves Two Women
programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the twins, they've both got a crush on the same guy: Roger!
programme.sub-title: When a Man Loves Two Women

programme.title: Sister, Sister: You Are So Beautiful
programme.desc: Curiosity about their birth parents leads the twins, Ray, and Lisa on a quest to learn more about the girls' past.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Gimme a Brake
programme.desc: Tempted by the freedom of a new car, Tia and Tamera soon find themselves on a collision course with disaster when their car drops off a dock and into Lake Erie!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Daddys Girl
programme.desc: Tamera learns she's never too old to be a daddy's girl when she finds herself jealous of the growing closeness between Ray and Tia.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Sis Boom Bah
programme.desc: In a bid to boost their popularity at school, the twins try out for the cheerleading squad -- and Ray tries his hand at coaching them.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Kid-Napped
programme.desc: When a new girl in school threatens Tia and Tamera's popularity, the twins join forces to win back their friends with the help of a music superstar.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Boy From the Hood
programme.desc: Tamera falls for Tia's childhood friend Darnell, who's all grown up and running with a fast crowd.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I'll be There
programme.desc: Tia takes a job in a '50s diner to get away from Tamera and is shocked when Tamera starts working there, too.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Ch-Ch-Ch-Changes
programme.desc: The twins are thrilled when Ray and Lisa decide to take their relationship to a more romantic level, but have second thoughts when Roger outlines the downsides of their parents dating.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Exposure
programme.desc: When Tia poses for Tamera's class picture, the gorgeous photo that results makes Tamera extremely popular, and Tia extremely resentful.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Some Like it Hockey
programme.desc: Determined to make the school's all-male hockey team, Tia and Tamera must disguise themselves as boys and pretend they're just two of the guys!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Bring on Debate
programme.desc: Ray feels left out by the close bond Tamera forms with her new music teacher.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Little Man Date
programme.desc: Tia and her new beau Kyle decide to fix up Tamera with Kyle's little brother – who turns out to be a lot littler than anyone thought!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Ski Squad
programme.desc: A ski trip designed to give the twins some independence from their parents has them longing for the comforts of home after they're trapped in an avalanche.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Cafeteria Lady
programme.desc: Lisa gets a new job in the high school cafeteria, but her outrageous behavior proves to be a major embarrassment to Tia.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Three the Heart Way
programme.desc: Dateless Tamera feels like a third wheel when Tia and Roger get matched up on Valentine's Day.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Model Tia
programme.desc: The family flashes back to the Disco Decade when Ray and Lisa attend a 1970s theme party, while Tamera falls prey to a man who promises to launch her modeling career.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: My Guy
programme.desc: A high school nerd out to woo Tia turns to Tamera for help in updating his image, but when his makeover is complete, Tamera realizes she wants to keep him for herself!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Dutch
programme.desc: When a girl from Tia's old neighborhood challenges her to a double Dutch jump rope contest, Tia becomes obsessed with winning.

programme.title: Flashpoint

programme.desc: Team One struggles with a secret that could compromise their drug bust and end Wordy's career.
